acceptance: SD-001 readiness probe section does not treat Redis as a required dependency. Redis check is documented as conditional (only when Redis is configured). The line includes a conditional qualifier like 'if configured'
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-001-system-architecture.md:1013-1016
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: SD-001 Section 10 defines the readiness probe as checking Redis connectivity (PING) and returning 503 if any critical dependency is unreachable. This treats Redis as a critical dependency, contradicting the '(optional, recommended)' annotation in the architecture diagram (line 63) and ADR-008 policy. A deployment running without Redis would fail readiness checks according to this spec.
Suggested fix: Update the readiness probe specification to make Redis check conditional: 'Checks Redis connectivity (PING) if Redis is configured.' Clarify that Redis is not a critical dependency — only PostgreSQL unavailability should trigger 503.
title: integration: SD-001 readiness probe contradicts Redis-optional policy (ADR-008)

acceptance: docker compose up completes migrate service without runtime npm install AND migrate service starts in under 5 seconds on a warm Docker cache
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docker-compose.yml:28
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The migrate docker service runs 'npm install -g graphile-migrate@1.4.1 --quiet' on every container start. This adds 10-30 seconds latency and a network dependency to every 'docker compose up'. With restart: on-failure, if npm is unreachable the container enters a restart loop adding noise and blocking the dev workflow.
Suggested fix: Build a small Dockerfile that pre-installs graphile-migrate (e.g. FROM node:20-alpine with RUN npm install -g graphile-migrate@1.4.1), or use the Bun-based server image to run migrations directly since graphile-migrate is already a project dependency.
title: integration: migrate service reinstalls graphile-migrate on every docker compose up

acceptance: graphile-migrate watch starts successfully with env vars from .env.example AND .env.example contains ROOT_DATABASE_URL
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: packages/server/.gmrc:1-4
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The .gmrc config has no rootConnectionString and .env.example has no ROOT_DATABASE_URL. The commit creates current.sql (implying the graphile-migrate watch dev workflow is intended), but 'graphile-migrate watch' requires rootConnectionString to create and manage shadow databases for migration validation. Without it, running 'graphile-migrate watch' fails with a connection error, making current.sql unusable for iterative migration development.
Suggested fix: Add ROOT_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://apogee:apogee_dev@localhost:5432/postgres to .env.example. Optionally add a shadowDatabaseUrl entry or document that ROOT_DATABASE_URL must point to a connection with CREATE DATABASE privileges.
title: integration: .gmrc missing rootConnectionString blocks graphile-migrate watch workflow

acceptance: ci-node job either executes tests on Node.js runtime (not bun test) or is explicitly documented as not providing runtime-level Node.js validation
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/ci.yml:54-107
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The ci-node job claims to be a Node.js LTS fallback that validates Node.js compatibility (per SD-003-WP0 dual-runtime requirement), but it runs 'bun test' (line 107) which always executes on Bun's runtime. Tests import from 'bun:test' (a Bun-only module) and cannot run under Node.js. The BUN_RUNTIME_TRANSPILER_CACHE_PATH env var only changes cache location, not runtime. The job is functionally identical to ci-bun, providing false confidence in Node.js compatibility.
Suggested fix: Either (a) create a parallel test setup using node --test or vitest that can run on Node.js for the shared package, or (b) scope the Node.js job to only lint and typecheck (which genuinely run on Node.js via npx), or (c) document the dual-runtime gap as a known limitation and create a follow-up task to implement true Node.js test execution.
title: correctness: ci-node job does not actually test Node.js runtime

Audit-log all revocations. See SD-004 section 7.4.

description: Implement OIDC authentication flow using openid-client. Routes: GET /auth/oidc/login (redirect to IdP), GET /auth/oidc/callback (exchange code, validate id_token, build IdentityAssertion, call resolveUser, create session). Supports Keycloak, Okta, Azure AD via discovery URL. State/nonce bound to signed cookie. See SD-004 sections 7.1, 9, 11. Accepts at least one OIDC provider in integration test.
title: PLT-006-C: OIDC SSO flow — openid-client + /auth/oidc/* routes

acceptance: packages/shared tests execute and pass using Node.js runtime (not bun test) in the ci-node CI job
description: Follow-on from hx-4dad2691. ADR-009 requires the application to remain compatible with Node.js as a fallback runtime, verified by CI running tests on both runtimes. Currently ci-node only validates lint and typecheck; it cannot run the test suite because tests import from 'bun:test' (Bun-only). To satisfy the dual-runtime requirement properly, the shared package tests need a Node.js-compatible test runner (e.g., vitest or node --test). Suggested approach: (a) add a vitest config for packages/shared that mirrors the existing bun:test suite, (b) run vitest in ci-node after Node.js setup, (c) update test imports to use a runtime-agnostic adapter or dual-import strategy. Spec: ADR-009, SD-003-WP0 dual-runtime CI pipeline deliverable.
title: feat: implement true Node.js runtime test execution for shared package

description: Implement TOTP MFA using otpauth library. Routes: GET /auth/mfa/setup (generate secret, return QR URI), POST /auth/mfa/setup/confirm (verify code, persist encrypted secret), POST /auth/mfa/verify (verify code during login, elevate session to mfaVerified=true). MFA enforcement: isMfaRequired() checks role config. Failed TOTP increments failed_login_count. See SD-004 sections 7.3, 12. Acceptance: unit tests for enrollment, verification, brute-force protection.
title: PLT-006-E: TOTP MFA — enrollment + verification + /auth/mfa/* routes

description: Implement E2E workflow tests using Fastify inject(): (1) Quote→order→compliance check→fulfill→ship→invoice→payment; (2) PO→goods receipt→three-way match→payment; (3) month-end close→IC eliminations→consolidated statements. Requires all prior WPs complete. Ref: SD-003-WP7.
title: WP7-E2E: Order-to-cash and procure-to-pay E2E workflow tests

acceptance: Running 'bun run --filter @apogee/server start' starts a Fastify server listening on a configurable port. Verified by: curl http://localhost:<port>/health/live returns 200.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: packages/server/src/index.ts
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: index.ts only re-exports buildApp and SERVER_VERSION constants. No app.listen() call exists anywhere in the server package. The dev and start scripts in package.json reference src/index.ts but running them produces no running server. The server cannot be started.
Suggested fix: Add a server startup block to index.ts (or a separate main.ts entrypoint) that calls buildApp() then app.listen() with port/host from environment variables (PORT, HOST). Update package.json scripts if a separate entrypoint is used.
title: correctness: server package has no entrypoint — cannot be started

description: Create graphile-migrate SQL migrations for: authn_sessions (session store), authn_identity_links (IdP identity to user mapping), and users table MFA columns (mfa_totp_secret, mfa_enabled, failed_login_count, locked_until). See SD-004 section 6 for full schema. Acceptance: migrations apply cleanly, indexes created.
title: PLT-006-A: DB migrations — authn_sessions, authn_identity_links, users MFA columns

acceptance: MoneyAmountSchema.parse('1234567890123.123456') succeeds (13 integer + 6 decimal = 19 digits) AND MoneyAmountSchema.parse('12345678901234') throws (14 integer digits exceeds limit) AND corresponding test exists in schemas.test.ts
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: packages/shared/src/schemas.ts:25
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: MoneyAmountSchema regex /^\d+(\.\d{1,6})?$/ enforces max 6 decimal places but places no upper bound on integer digits. NUMERIC(19,6) per ADR-003 allows max 13 integer digits (19 total - 6 decimal). Amounts like '12345678901234567890' (20 digits) pass schema validation but would be rejected by PostgreSQL. Validation should fail fast at the schema layer.
Suggested fix: Change regex to /^\d{1,13}(\.\d{1,6})?$/ to enforce the 13-integer-digit limit from NUMERIC(19,6). Add a test for a 14+ integer-digit amount that should be rejected.
title: correctness: MoneyAmountSchema missing NUMERIC(19,6) integer-digit limit

description: Add WP-2 Finance tables to current.sql: fiscal_year, fiscal_period, account, account_segment, account_segment_value, account_mapping, journal_entry, journal_entry_line, period_status, gl_balance, vendor_bill, vendor_bill_line, payment_batch, payment, vendor_bill_payment, customer_invoice, customer_invoice_line, customer_payment, payment_application, dunning_run, dunning_letter, currency, exchange_rate_type, exchange_rate, intercompany_agreement, intercompany_transaction, elimination_entry. Fix purchase_order_line.account_id FK. AR tables use plain UUID stubs for customer_id/sales_order_id (WP-5). Audit triggers on vendor_bill, customer_invoice, payment, customer_payment. Ref: SD-002-data-model.md §3.1 (fiscal), §4.
title: FIN-DB: WP-2 Finance DB schema migration

description:
Create Zod schemas in packages/shared/src/entity-schemas/ per SD-002-data-model.md §6 and §7.

Files: sales.ts (customer, product, price-list, quote, sales-order, RMA schemas), crm.ts (crm-company, crm-contact, pipeline-stage, opportunity, activity, lead schemas).

Export from entity-schemas/index.ts. Tests in packages/shared/test/sales-crm-schemas.test.ts.

Acceptance: CreateCustomerSchema, CreateProductSchema, CreateQuoteSchema (replaces existing stub), CreateSalesOrderSchema, CreateReturnAuthorizationSchema, CreateCrmCompanySchema, CreateCrmContactSchema, CreateOpportunitySchema, CreateActivitySchema, CreateLeadSchema all validate per acceptance criteria in FEAT-003.
title: WP5-ZOD: Sales & CRM Zod schemas — customer, product, quotes, orders, RMA, CRM

acceptance:
- Generic PostgreSQL trigger function audit_stamp() inserts into audit_entry on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE for any business table
- setAuditContext(pool, actorId, actorEmail, entityId) application helper executes SET LOCAL before a query
- Integration test: insert to a stamped table -> audit_entry row present with correct actor/action/resource
- Passes full quality gate
title: PLT-003: Audit log trigger infrastructure — reusable PG trigger + actor context

description: Implement SAML 2.0 SP flow using @node-saml/node-saml. Routes: GET /auth/saml/login (generate AuthnRequest, redirect to IdP SSO URL), POST /auth/saml/acs (ACS endpoint — validate SAMLResponse signature + conditions, build IdentityAssertion, call resolveUser, create session). See SD-004 sections 7.2, 9, 11. Acceptance: SAMLResponse validation with known test fixture.
title: PLT-006-D: SAML 2.0 SSO flow — @node-saml/node-saml + /auth/saml/* routes

description: Implement background job that refreshes near-expiry OIDC access tokens using stored refresh tokens. Job runs every 10 minutes via Graphile Worker cron. Refresh tokens stored AES-256-GCM encrypted in authn_sessions.idp_refresh_token_enc. Key from APP_SESSION_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var. See SD-004 sections 7.5, 12. Acceptance: job exchanges refresh token and updates stored token without touching session expiry.
title: PLT-006-G: OIDC refresh token background job (Graphile Worker)

description: Implement SessionManager (PostgreSQL-backed) and registerSessionAuthHook Fastify middleware. SessionManager: create, load, touch, revoke, revokeAll, list. Middleware: extract apogee_session cookie, load session, enforce inactivity timeout (30 min), touch on every request, attach req.user + req.session. Requires @fastify/cookie. See SD-004 sections 5.3, 5.4, 7.4, 10. Acceptance: session expiry, revocation, and multi-session list tested.
title: PLT-006-F: SessionManager implementation + session auth middleware

acceptance:
- entityContext Fastify plugin: reads entity_id from JWT claim or X-Entity-Id header
- requirePermission() hook: checks user has permission code for current entity
- ITAR compartment check: rejects requests where user lacks compartment access
- Unit tests: entity isolation, permission denial, ITAR enforcement
title: PLT-002: RBAC engine — entity-context middleware, permission check, ITAR compartment enforcement

acceptance: docker-compose.yml contains an explicit instruction (comment or documentation reference) explaining how to start the local dev stack without Redis, e.g., 'docker compose up postgres migrate'
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docker-compose.yml:34-36
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Issue erp-8028b91d (AR-003) acceptance criteria states 'docker-compose.yml documents Redis as optional with instructions for running without it.' The commit added a comment explaining Redis is optional and describing fallback behavior, but does not include actual instructions for running the stack without Redis (e.g., 'docker compose up postgres migrate'). The issue was closed despite this gap.
Suggested fix: Add a comment line to docker-compose.yml such as '# To run without Redis: docker compose up postgres migrate' or document this in a .env.example or README.
title: correctness: docker-compose.yml missing run-without-Redis instructions per AR-003 acceptance criteria

description: Full OIDC SSO (Keycloak, Okta, Azure AD) + SAML 2.0 SSO, TOTP MFA, session management, token refresh, forced logout. WP-0 delivered JWT validation scaffolding; this delivers the full AuthN requirement. Needs design pass before implementation — provider abstraction layer not yet designed.
title: PLT-006: AuthN — OIDC SSO integration, SAML 2.0, TOTP MFA, session management
